summary: Matched the Entei item implementation by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itentei.s with src/melee/it/items/itentei.c, switching the build from the assembly object to the C object, and adding the small item-specific attribute/variable typing needed for the C to compile and match. The PR had no body, comments, or reviews in the slice, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.

key_files.role: Deleted 345-line assembly source after matching it in C.
Added the matched C implementation for Entei item functions, state table, callbacks, effect spawning, sound behavior, and physics/state transitions.
Expanded declarations for all local Entei functions and the state table.
Added ItEnteiVars entei to the Item xDD4_itemVar union.
Added prototype for it_8026E248 used by Entei's ground/air transition callback.
Added prototype for it_80279C48 used during Entei initialization.
Changed itentei.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Changed the linked object from asm/melee/it/items/itentei.s.o to src/melee/it/items/itentei.c.o.

what_changed: Replaced the Entei assembly file with a 165-line C source file implementing functions it_802CF3E0 through it_802CF7EC and data symbol it_803F7DC8.
Defined ItemStateTable it_803F7DC8 in C as two state rows: one using it_802CF544/it_802CF640/it_802CF67C and one using it_802CF7A8/it_802CF7CC/it_802CF7EC.

Initialization it_802CF3E0 now calls it_80279C48, clears xDAC_itcmd_var0/xDB0_itcmd_var1/xDB4_itcmd_var2, applies a special attribute float through it_80279CDC, and enters the initial state via it_802CF744.
Added Entei special attributes with fields f32 x0 and s32 timer, and item vars with padding to offset 0xE34 plus an s32 timer.
Mapped raw access at item variable offset 0xE34 to it->xDD4_itemVar.entei.timer by adding ItEnteiVars to the Item union.
Implemented effect and audio behavior: efSync_Spawn(0x468), efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll hitlag callbacks, efLib_DestroyAll cleanup, and Item_8026AE84 sound calls including 0x2741 through 0x2744.
Implemented random periodic Entei sound selection with HSD_Randi(3) and a reset from the Entei attribute timer.
Implemented ground/air behavior using GA_Air checks, it_80272860 fall physics, it_8026E248 for air, and it_8026D62C for ground.
Updated configure.py and obj_files.mk so the matched C object is built instead of the old assembly object.
Added missing external prototypes for it_8026E248 and it_80279C48 needed by the new C source.
smart_moves: Introduced a named ItEnteiVars overlay for the xDD4 item-var union instead of continuing to access the raw 0xE34 item field directly.
Kept the special attributes minimal and evidence-based: only f32 x0 and s32 timer are named from observed accesses.
Used existing item infrastructure names and fields such as xDAC_itcmd_var0, xDB0_itcmd_var1, xDB4_itcmd_var2, entered_hitlag, exited_hitlag, on_accessory, xC4_article_data, and xDD4_itemVar.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack layout with PAD_STACK(16) in it_802CF3E0 and PAD_STACK(8) in functions that needed it.
Converted the assembly data table directly into an ItemStateTable initializer, keeping callback ordering clear and searchable.
Made the build-system switch in both configure.py and obj_files.mk, which is necessary for fully removing the assembly source from the active build.
decomp_lessons: When matching item files, replace the asm object in obj_files.mk and mark the corresponding source as Matching in configure.py in the same PR.
For item-specific data stored under xDD4_itemVar, add a typed union member once the offset is understood; here 0xE34 became xDD4_itemVar.entei.timer.
Small item-specific attribute structs should be added only for proven fields; unnamed or unclear fields can remain generic, as with itEnteiAttributes.x0.
Callback-heavy item files often need both local item headers and subsystem headers for camera, effects, random, and item helper functions.
Assembly callback tables can usually be translated into ItemStateTable arrays if the function pointer order is preserved exactly.
Do not omit no-op callbacks: it_802CF44C is an empty return function but is still passed as a transition callback to it_8026E248 and it_8026D62C.
Hitlag effect behavior is represented by assigning entered_hitlag and exited_hitlag callbacks, not by explicit pause/resume calls at each effect site.
Matching may require explicit PAD_STACK even in otherwise straightforward C functions.
naming_conventions: Item-specific variable structs use the It<ItemName>Vars pattern, shown by ItEnteiVars.
The Entei special attributes typedef is named itEnteiAttributes in this PR, with lowercase initial 'it' matching the added diff.
The Item union member for item-specific vars is lowercase item name, shown by xDD4_itemVar.entei.
Function names remained address-based, such as it_802CF3E0 and it_802CF544; no semantic renames were introduced.
State table symbol remained address-based as it_803F7DC8.
Header prototypes in this slice generally preserve address comments where they existed in shared headers, while itentei.h declarations were expanded without address comments.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the assembly file only after the C version could replace it as a Matching object.
Used PAD_STACK(16) in it_802CF3E0 to reproduce the original 0x30-byte stack frame behavior implied by the assembly.
Used PAD_STACK(8) in it_802CF470 and it_802CF6C8 for stack-sensitive matching.
Represented the sdata2 constant it_804DD420 as a local f32 v = 1.0f passed by address to efSync_Spawn.
Translated the assembly random branch ladder into switch (HSD_Randi(3)) with cases 0, 1, and 2 selecting sfx IDs 0x2742, 0x2743, and 0x2744.
Matched item variable offset 0xE34 by defining ItEnteiVars with padding[0xE34 - 0xDD4] followed by s32 timer.
Preserved callback signatures and bool return behavior for item state callbacks: it_802CF544, it_802CF67C, it_802CF7A8, and it_802CF7EC.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs that add xDD4_itemVar union members with padding to known offsets.
Find item matching PRs that translate ItemStateTable data from assembly to C.
Find uses of PAD_STACK in matched item files around effect spawning or initialization.
Find other legendary Pokemon item files near itraikou, itsuikun, and itkireihana for similar attribute structs and timer behavior.
Find PRs that added prototypes to it_266F.h or it_2725.h as part of item file matching.
